notes: Dark mode inverts highlight.js colors vs light: in light hljs-attr=olive-green(rgb(92,148,13)) & hljs-string=blue(rgb(24,100,171)); in dark hljs-attr=light-blue(rgb(165,216,255)) & hljs-string=green(rgb(64,192,87)). Both directions readable but the semantic role of each class is visually swapped between themes — keys are blue in dark/green in light, strings are green in dark/blue in light. Not strictly inconsistent but worth flagging for design-token review.
Nav background rgb(65,73,81) is identical in both light and dark themes — a fixed dark slate header. Good for brand consistency across themes.
Link color in dark mode is pure white rgb(255,255,255) — no hue differentiation from body text rgb(201,201,201) other than lightness. Links rely on underline to be distinguishable.

TD border-color in dark mode is rgb(201,201,201) which equals body foreground — dividers are quite prominent. In light mode dividers were lighter grey. Visual weight of table rows differs between themes.
